title: 2026 U.S. Senate race in Delaware
description: This race is for Delaware’s Class II seat in the U.S. Senate, with the general election scheduled for November 3, 2026. It matters because it will help determine control of the Senate in the next Congress, though Delaware is widely rated as a strongly Democratic state and the seat is currently held by incumbent Democrat Chris Coons. The main contrast is between Coons’ long tenure and bipartisan governing style and the challengers’ efforts to present alternatives in a state where Democrats have a clear registration and electoral advantage.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Chris Coons
candidates.summary: Chris Coons is the incumbent U.S. senator from Delaware, first elected in 2010. His official biography describes him as a pragmatic, bipartisan lawmaker who previously served in New Castle County government and worked in the private sector before entering the Senate.

career_history.title: U.S. Senator
career_history.description: Represents Delaware in the Senate; serves on Appropriations, Foreign Relations, Judiciary, Small Business and Entrepreneurship, and Ethics committees.

career_history.title: New Castle County Executive
career_history.description: Led county government for six years before election to the Senate.

career_history.title: New Castle County Council President
career_history.description: Served four years as council president.

career_history.title: Employee
career_history.description: Worked for the Delaware-based manufacturing company before entering county government.

candidates.name: Christopher Beardsley
candidates.summary: Christopher Beardsley is a Democratic primary challenger to Chris Coons in Delaware’s 2026 Senate race. His campaign website says he is running on issues including healthcare, housing, education, wages, and social justice, and social media bios describe him as a former HUD analyst and union member.

summary_sources.title: Beardsley | Join the Movement for Delaware
Issues | Take Action Now for Change — Beardsley

career_history.title: HUD analyst
career_history.organization: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
career_history.description: Listed in campaign materials and social media bios as a former HUD analyst.

career_history.title: Union member
career_history.description: Described in campaign materials/social bios as an AFGE union member.

candidates.name: Michael Katz
candidates.summary: Michael Katz is running in the Republican primary for Delaware’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. His campaign materials and social media present him as a physician, business owner, and problem-solver, but publicly available biographical detail is limited in the sources reviewed.

summary_sources.title: Dr. Mike Katz For U.S. Senate
Dr. Mike Katz Republican for U.S. Senate - Delaware (@drmikekatz)

career_history.title: Physician
career_history.description: Campaign materials describe him as a physician, including pediatric critical care training.

career_history.title: Business owner
career_history.description: Campaign materials describe him as a business owner.

candidates.name: John Shulli
candidates.summary: John Shulli is a Republican candidate in Delaware’s 2026 Senate primary. His campaign website frames his candidacy around an “American Renaissance” theme focused on prosperity, technology, jobs, and restoring civic confidence.

candidates.name: Dexter Bland
candidates.summary: Dexter Bland is listed by Ballotpedia as an independent general-election candidate in Delaware’s 2026 U.S. Senate race. Publicly available campaign material is sparse, but his social media and committee branding suggest a community- and advocacy-oriented campaign focused on education, democracy, and local issues.

summary_sources.title: Believe In Bland (@believeinbland) • Instagram photos and videos
